title: orlaya pumila
intro: Orlaya pumila, also known as baby's breath or lesser meadow rue, is a perennial plant in the carrot family that grows to be around 2-4 feet tall and has delicate white flowers. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Orlaya pumila include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate menstrual cramps, and soothe digestive issues. Orlaya pumila can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health conditions.
